summary: This bundle records deterministic proof for the evaluation-process guide's compare-worktree and executor-variant artifact contracts.

evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the machine-readable baseline/candidate path handoff and structured review variant schema/output-file handling are marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Every human reviewer actually uses the emitted paths
Every external executor is available in every environment
Every possible review schema is semantically correct

notableAssertions: the helper emits JSON with outputDir, baseline.path, and candidate.path
baseline and candidate worktrees contain the expected checked-out files
CAUTILUS_RUN_DIR and explicit output-dir precedence are visible in the emitted paths
review variants accepts prompt/schema/output-file driven executor templates
default schema files can flow from review prompt input into variant execution
pass, concern, blocked, and failed executor outcomes are classified without prose-only guessing
successful variant outputs are lifted when a mixed executor set has partial success

decision.reason: The cited tests prove the guide's artifact handoff contract: compare worktrees emit machine-readable paths, and review variants consume schema/output-file driven executor contracts into structured pass, concern, blocked, or failed packets.
